title: parse_proxy_addr: IPv6 brackets break host string
description: parse_proxy_addr extracts the host string from socks5://[::1]:9050 as "[::1]" (with brackets). This is then passed to Socks5Stream::connect(("[::1]", 9050), ...). Inside tokio-socks, IntoTargetAddr for (&str, u16) first tries self.0.parse::<IpAddr>() — which fails for "[::1]" because Rust's IpAddr parser does not accept RFC-2396 bracket notation. tokio-socks then falls through to the domain name path and treats "[::1]" as a literal hostname, causing a DNS lookup failure or proxy rejection. The fix is to strip brackets before returning: let host = host.trim_start_matches('[').trim_end_matches(']'); This is a well-known edge case documented in RFC 3986 §3.2.2. A test case with socks5://[::1]:9050 would have caught this.
